title: Training Philosophy
description: A source's non-negotiable guiding principles. Governs what the system will and won't do when drawing from this methodology.

id.description: Unique slug. Used as reference key across the system. e.g. 'starting_strength', 'uphill_athlete'

name.description: Human-readable name. e.g. 'Starting Strength / Mark Rippetoe'

core_principles.description: Non-negotiable beliefs expressed as short slug strings. These are the axioms — the system won't violate them when this source is active.

scope.description: Which modalities this philosophy claims authority over. Use existing modality IDs or introduce new ones specific to this package (e.g. 'swimming', 'cycling', 'contact_strength').

bias.description: Modalities this philosophy actively emphasizes. Subset of scope.

avoid_with.description: Modality IDs this philosophy conflicts with at high volume. Can reference any installed modality ID.

required_equipment.description: Equipment that must be present for this philosophy to be applicable. If missing, system cannot draw from this source.

intensity_model.description: How this philosophy approaches intensity across the week.

progression_philosophy.description: Primary mechanism by which this source defines progress.

sources.description: Source documents or URLs this entry was derived from.

notes.description: Free-text notes, known gaps, or caveats about this entry.

frameworks_are_phases.description: DEPRECATED: Use framework_groups instead. When true, frameworks from this philosophy are sequential phases (user gets 'Full Program' option that includes all phases in order). When false/omitted, frameworks are alternatives for different goals/styles (user picks one).

primary_framework_id.description: Default framework ID for this philosophy. Used when no specific framework is selected or as the starting phase for phased frameworks.

framework_groups.description: Defines how frameworks are grouped and presented to users. Each group is either alternatives (pick one) or sequential (all phases in order). Replaces frameworks_are_phases pattern.

id.description: Unique group identifier within this philosophy

name.description: Human-readable group name shown to user (e.g., 'Year-Round Training', '4-Month Periodization')

type.description: alternatives = user picks one framework; sequential = all frameworks run as phases

frameworks.description: Framework IDs belonging to this group. Must reference valid framework files.

canonical_phase_sequence.description: Required when type=sequential. Defines phase progression with framework_id per phase.

weeks.description: Duration of this phase in weeks

framework_id.description: Framework to use for this phase. Must be in group's frameworks array.

focus.description: Optional description of this phase's training focus

priority_override.description: Optional modality priority adjustments specific to this phase

canonical_phase_sequence.description: DEPRECATED: Move into framework_groups[].canonical_phase_sequence for sequential groups. Standard periodization phases for this philosophy. Required when frameworks_are_phases is true.

weeks.description: Duration of this phase in weeks

framework_id.description: Phase-specific framework override. Required when frameworks_are_phases is true.

focus.description: Optional description of this phase's training focus

priority_override.description: Optional modality priority adjustments specific to this phase
